name: Covarrubias and Hortiguela from Salas de los Infantes — Natural Park Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla — circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route
name_original: Covarrubias y Hortigüela desde Salas de los Infantes — Parque Natural Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla — circular por el Camino del Cid Cicloturista
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Salas de los Infantes are situated within the Sierra de la Demanda region of Burgos, Spain. The area features varied terrain, including majestic oak groves, extensive pine forests, and routes along the River Arlanza. Cyclists encounter significant elevation changes, with mountain passes like Puerto del Manquillo, and unique geological formations. This landscape provides diverse conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Salas De Los Infantes The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic journey through the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Covarrubias and Hortiguela from Salas de los Infantes — Natural Park Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla — circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route, a difficult 38.3 miles (61.7 km) path. This route explores the Natural Park Sabinares de Arlanza and incorporates sections of the Camino del Cid. Local touring cyclists also love the Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Sad Hill, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail leading through the historic Sad Hill Cemetery and offering views of Peña de Carazo, often completed in about 7 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Salas de los Infantes is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, ancient oak groves, and routes along the Arlanza river. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Salas de los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Salas de los Infantes?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Salas de los Infantes, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are situated within the Sierra de la Demanda region, known for its varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Salas de los Infantes?
A: The terrain around Salas de los Infantes is quite varied. You can expect to cycle through majestic oak groves and extensive pine forests, along the scenic River Arlanza, and encounter significant elevation changes, including mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations and picturesque views of the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Salas de los Infantes?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy touring cycling routes available. While many routes are more challenging, these easier options allow beginners and families to enjoy the region's natural beauty at a more relaxed pace. For a scenic journey, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027937673/sierra-de-la-demanda-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can discover a wealth of natural features and landmarks. Routes often pass through ancient oak groves, offer views of the Sierra de la Demanda, and follow the River Arlanza. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Carazo Plateau&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique geological formations like fossil trees.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore while cycling in the region?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. Many routes incorporate historical points of interest such as the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, a filming location for 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly'. You can also find ruins of monasteries like the Monasterio de San Pedro de Arlanza, hermitages, and the Castrovido castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027910716/covarrubias-and-hortiguela-from-salas-de-los-infantes-natural-park-sabinares-de-arlanza-la-yecla-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarrubias and Hortiguela route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, incorporates sections of the historic Camino del Cid.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Salas de los Infantes offers numerous challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists, with 15 routes rated as difficult. These often feature significant elevation gains and long distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Sad Hill&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 73 km with substantial climbing, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons – San Francisco Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling around Salas de los Infantes?
A: The region offers diverse conditions, making it suitable for cycling across different seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for exploring the oak groves and forests. Summer can be warm, but routes along the River Arlanza or through shaded pine forests offer cooler options. For challenging mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;, warmer months are often preferred.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Salas de los Infantes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027937673/sierra-de-la-demanda-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027910716/covarrubias-and-hortiguela-from-salas-de-los-infantes-natural-park-sabinares-de-arlanza-la-yecla-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarrubias and Hortiguela from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Salas de los Infantes?
A: Touring cyclists frequently praise the region's varied landscape, from the majestic oak groves and pine forests to the challenging mountain passes. The historical and cultural points of interest, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, are also highly appreciated. The well-established network of trails caters to different skill levels, making it a rewarding destination for many.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Carazo Plateau&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas, and there's a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt; highlight. Routes often climb to higher elevations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate sections of the Camino del Cid?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in the area integrate sections of the historic Camino del Cid. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027910716/covarrubias-and-hortiguela-from-salas-de-los-infantes-natural-park-sabinares-de-arlanza-la-yecla-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarrubias and Hortiguela from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; route explores the Natural Park Sabinares de Arlanza and includes parts of this famous long-distance trail.

Q: Are there any routes that feature unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique geological features. Some routes may lead you past areas with 'fossil trees' (macrófósiles) and the spectacular rocky formations of 'Las Calderas', offering an interesting natural attraction alongside your ride.
tips: The Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos is considered to be one of the most culturally and historically significant monasteries in Spain. If you are lucky, during a visit to the monastery you will be able to enjoy the world-famous Gregorian chants of the monks, who still cultivate and practice them today as ever. An album released by the monks was in the US charts for 53 weeks and went platinum three times.

A very interesting town with perfectly preserved historical heritage. Highly recommended to see the traditional wooden beam houses. The entire town of Covarrubias has been declared an Asset of Cultural Interest since 1965. Of its heritage it is worth highlighting the Torreón de Fernán González, the only Castilian fortress from before the 11th century that is preserved.
Available benches, water source and place to make a barbecue. Possible camping.

waypoints.name: Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos
